name: ma'at (justice he upholds)
shortDescription: Ma'at is the ancient Egyptian concept of truth, balance, order, law, morality, and justice. She represents the fundamental order of the universe, established at creation.
description: Ma'at is the ancient Egyptian concept of truth, balance, order, law, morality, and justice. She embodies the fundamental order of the universe, established at creation to distinguish the cosmos from chaos. As a goddess, Ma'at is often depicted as a woman with an ostrich feather on her head, sometimes winged. The feather is the symbol of Ma'at and is used in the Weighing of the Heart ceremony in the afterlife. In this ceremony, the deceased's heart is weighed against the feather of Ma'at. If the heart is lighter than the feather, the deceased is deemed worthy to enter the afterlife. If the heart is heavier, weighed down by sin and wrongdoing, it is devoured by Ammit, the Devourer of Souls. Ma'at is not only a goddess but also a principle that pharaohs were expected to uphold. They were seen as the maintainers of Ma'at on Earth, ensuring cosmic balance and societal harmony. The concept of Ma'at permeated all aspects of Egyptian life, from law and governance to personal morality and religious beliefs. Maintaining Ma'at was crucial for the well-being of both the individual and society as a whole. Her importance is underscored by her presence throughout Egyptian history and mythology, solidifying her role as a cornerstone of their civilization.
